title: p-series
description: Determine whether a given p-series is convergent or divergent.

assessment_items.question_content:
$1+\dfrac{1}{2^5}+\dfrac{1}{3^5}+\dfrac{1}{4^5}+...+\dfrac{1}{n^5}+...$

**Is the series convergent or divergent?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
This is a $p$-series, because it's of the general form $\displaystyle\sum\limits_{n=1}^{\infty}\dfrac{1}{n^{^p}}$.

Specifically, this is a $p$-series where $p=5$.
$p$-series are convergent for $p>1$ and divergent for $0<p\leq1$. [[☃ explanation 1]]
In conclusion, the series is convergent.
